Title: Community Outreach App
Description: Mobile app connecting volunteers with local community needs
ExtendedDescription:
# System Design — Vibe Mobile Coding Platform

## 1) Problem & Goals

**Problem.** Traditional code editors and CI tooling don’t fit mobile-first workflows; spinning up dev environments, sharing reproducible demos, and collaborating on the go is slow and inconsistent.

**Goal.** Vibe provides a mobile-native, cloud-backed coding environment with AI assistance, secure sandboxes, live previews, and one‑tap sharing. The experience must be fast, resilient, collaborative, and secure-by-default.

**Guiding principles.** Mobile-first UX, ephemeral-by-default compute, collaboration as a primitive, guardrails for cost & safety, strong observability, and privacy‑aware AI.

---

## 2) Personas & Primary Use Cases

* **Solo dev:** explore templates, prototype APIs/UI, deploy a demo quickly.
* **Team lead:** create a workspace, seed tasks, review PRs, comment inline.
* **Student/beginner:** learn by forking sandboxes, step-by-step AI guidance.
* **SRE/on-call:** reproduce issues with a pinned environment snapshot.

Key flows: create sandbox from template → code in mobile editor → AI assist → run/tests → share preview link → commit/PR → deploy.

---

## 3) High‑Level Architecture

```
Mobile App (React Native)
  ↕ GraphQL/REST + WebSocket (SignalR/Azure Web PubSub)
API Gateway / BFF (Edge auth, rate limiting)
  ├─ Identity & Org Service (Azure AD B2C)
  ├─ Projects & Templates Service (PostgreSQL)
  ├─ Sandbox Orchestrator (Kubernetes/Container Apps + ACI)
  ├─ File/Repo Service (Blob Storage + Git mirror)
  ├─ AI Assist Service (Azure OpenAI / OpenAI, prompt policy)
  ├─ Build & CI Service (Queues + Runners)
  └─ Notifications/Events (Service Bus → WebSocket)
Shared: Redis cache, Observability (App Insights + Grafana), Secrets (Key Vault)
Artifacts: Blob Storage (code, logs), Container Registry (ACR)
```

**Cloud mapping (Azure-first).**

* Compute: **Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)** or **Azure Container Apps** for services; **Azure Container Instances (ACI)** for ephemeral sandboxes.
* Data: **Azure Database for PostgreSQL** (metadata), **Blob Storage** (files, logs), **Redis** (Azure Cache) for sessions/cache.
* Messaging: **Azure Service Bus** (events, job queue).
* Realtime: **Azure Web PubSub** or **Azure SignalR Service**.
* Auth: **Azure AD B2C** (social/email), roles & orgs stored in Postgres.
* AI: **Azure OpenAI** (rate‑limited, PII‑safe prompting).
* Observability: **Application Insights**, **Log Analytics**, dashboards via **Grafana**.

---

## 4) Core Services (Responsibilities)

1. **API Gateway/BFF**: Edge auth (JWT), request shaping, rate limits, fan‑out to domain services. Exposes REST/GraphQL to the app.
2. **Identity & Org Service**: Users, orgs, teams, roles (Owner, Maintainer, Contributor, Viewer), SSO via AD B2C, token minting, audit trails.
3. **Projects & Templates Service**: Projects, sandboxes, templates, environment variables (scoped & encrypted), billing/quotas, run history.
4. **File/Repo Service**: Editing, versioned files, Git mirror (optional), diff/patch APIs, code search, large file support (Blob + signed URLs).
5. **Sandbox Orchestrator**: Provisions ephemeral containers/VMs with CPU/RAM caps, seccomp, read-only base images, per-sandbox volumes, ports exposure with TTL, snapshot & resume.
6. **Build & CI Service**: Runs builds/tests in workers, caches deps, artifacts to Blob, status events to clients.
7. **AI Assist Service**: Code completion, explain/fix, doc lookup, synthetic test generation; content policy enforcement and token budgeting.
8. **Notifications/Events**: Event bus → fan-out to WebSocket topics (sandbox state, build status, comments, mentions).

---

## 5) Data Model (high level)

* **User(id, email, name, provider, created\_at)**
* **Org(id, name, plan, owner\_id)** → many **Team(id, org\_id, name)**
* **Membership(user\_id, org\_id, role)**; **TeamMember(team\_id, user\_id, role)**
* **Project(id, org\_id, name, template\_id, repo\_url?, created\_by, visibility, created\_at)**
* **Sandbox(id, project\_id, status, image\_ref, cpu, memory, port\_map, ttl, last\_heartbeat)**
* **EnvVar(id, project\_id, key, value\_encrypted, scope, version)** (Key Vault-backed)
* **File(id, project\_id, path, sha, size, storage\_url, updated\_at)**
* **Build(id, project\_id, sandbox\_id?, status, logs\_url, duration\_ms, created\_at)**
* **Event(id, type, actor\_id, project\_id, payload\_json, created\_at)**

Indexes: project/org scopes, recent sandboxes, build status, path lookup; GIN on JSONB payloads for events.

---

## 6) Mobile App Architecture (React Native)

* **State**: Redux Toolkit or Zustand + React Query for server data.
* **Offline**: local cache (MMKV/SQLite) for files, recent logs; reconcile on reconnect.
* **Editor**: Monaco-based RN wrapper or code‑friendly text component with syntax highlighting, vim/Emacs keymaps toggle.
* **Realtime**: WebSocket client for sandbox/build events; optimistic edits with conflict resolution via CRDT (e.g., Yjs) for collaboration.
* **Previews**: In‑app webview bound to sandbox URL; secure token appended; auto‑refresh on rebuild.
* **Security**: Device storage encryption for tokens; biometric unlock; deep links for sharing read‑only previews.

---

## 7) Sandbox & Execution Model

* **Images**: Curated base images (node, python, dotnet) hardened with minimal packages; pinned digests.
* **Provisioning**: Job → Orchestrator picks region/node → spins ACI/Pod with resource limits, network policy, no privileged mode.
* **File sync**: Files fetched from Blob/Git on start; incremental writes stream back via API (chunked upload + signed URL).
* **Networking**: Each sandbox gets a random external port through an ingress gateway (NGINX/Envoy) with tokenized path; idle timeouts (e.g., 30–60 min).
* **Persistence**: Short‑lived volumes; snapshot on demand to Blob for resume.
* **Cost safety**: TTL enforced; quotas per org; background janitor closes idle sandboxes.

---

## 8) AI Assistance & Policy

* **Capabilities**: autocomplete, inline explain/fix, refactor hints, commit message drafting, test generation, README scaffolding.
* **Context**: Pull only necessary file windows + symbol index; hash‑based cache to avoid resending unchanged content.
* **Safety**: Prompt templates strip secrets; block transmit of .env and private keys; allow-list file patterns.
* **Controls**: Per‑org token budget, rate limits, audit logs per request (model, tokens, latency, outcome).

---

## 9) APIs & Real‑time Contracts

* **REST/GraphQL**: `/projects`, `/sandboxes`, `/files`, `/builds`, `/templates`, `/envvars`.
* **WebSocket topics**: `project:{id}:builds`, `sandbox:{id}:status`, `comments:{file}`.
* **Auth**: OAuth2/OIDC with AD B2C; JWT (15m) + refresh tokens; org/role claims inside JWT; fine-grained checks server-side.
* **Rate limits**: Per‑IP and per‑user (leaky‑bucket in Redis). Burst limits on sandbox creation and AI calls.

---

## 10) Security & Compliance

* **Isolation**: Namespaced sandboxes; seccomp, no root; read-only base FS; egress restrictions by default; per‑sandbox service account.
* **Secrets**: All env vars in Key Vault; injected at runtime; never persisted to code storage.
* **Transport**: TLS 1.2+ end‑to‑end; WAF at ingress (rate limiting, bot rules).
* **RBAC**: Owner/Maintainer/Contributor/Viewer; resource‑scoped tokens for preview links; signed URLs for logs/artifacts.
* **Compliance**: Audit logs (who/what/when) as immutable events; configurable data retention.

---

## 11) Observability & SRE

* **Metrics**: p95 editor latency, sandbox start time, AI latency/tokens, build success rate, quota utilization.
* **Tracing**: OpenTelemetry across BFF/services/runners; propagate trace IDs to logs.
* **Logging**: Structured JSON; per‑sandbox log stream; retention/archival policies.
* **SLOs**: 99.5% API availability; sandbox start < 20s p95; build queue wait < 30s p95.
* **Runbooks**: Hotfix path, sandbox drain, rolling restarts, region failover.

---

## 12) Scalability & Performance

* **Horizontally scale** stateless services behind an autoscaling gateway; cache hot reads in Redis.
* **Sharding** projects by org to reduce noisy neighbor effects.
* **Pre‑warm pools** of sandboxes for popular templates to hit cold‑start SLAs.
* **Async I/O** everywhere (uploads, builds); backpressure via Service Bus.
* **Edge caching** for read‑only assets and docs.

---

## 13) Deployment & Environments

* Envs: **dev**, **staging**, **prod**; infra as code via Bicep/Terraform; GitHub Actions pipelines.
* Strategy: blue‑green for BFF/services; rolling for workers; canary for AI prompts/policies.
* Secrets/Config: Key Vault + App Configuration; per‑env ACR images with immutable tags.

---

## 14) Risks & Mitigations

* **Cost blow‑ups** (sandbox sprawl): hard TTLs, org quotas, cost dashboards, alerts.
* **Sandbox escape**: keep kernel updated, strict policies, periodic penetration tests.
* **Data exfiltration via AI**: redaction, allow‑lists, audit & human review for flagged events.
* **Mobile editor UX**: invest in performant diffing, on-device caching, smart keyboard shortcuts.

---

## 15) MVP Scope & Phased Roadmap

**MVP (8–10 weeks):** login, projects/templates, single‑container sandboxes, file edit/save, run/start/stop, live preview, basic AI explain/fix, builds for Node/React templates, shareable read-only previews, metrics & logs.

**Phase 2:** collaboration cursors & comments, PR integration (GitHub), test runners, Python/.NET templates, secure org env vars, basic billing.

**Phase 3:** multi-container dev (db sidecars), pre-warmed pools, workspace recording/replay, advanced AI (refactors/tests/Doc Q\&A), enterprise SSO & policies, regional failover.

Title: מערכת חכמה לבריאות הנפש
Description: יצירת רשימת המתנה "חכמה" של הפונים שמאפשרת יכולות סינון והתאמת מטפל למטופל, יצירת קבוצות טיפוליות ועוד, וחיבור רשימת ההמתנה לאתר הקיים.
ExtendedDescription:
כיום, המענה הנוכחי לפניות  ידני ברובו ודורש מעבר איכותני של מורשים מטעם העמותה, כדי לייצר תובנה קלינית על אופן הטיפול הנחוץ.

 התהליך הנוכחי הינו:


יש פניה של מטופלים (מופנים מקופות החולים או פרטיים) אל מזכירות העמותה, שם המטרה לקצר תהליכים ככל הניתן, לא לפתוח תיק מטופל או להאריך בדיבור אלא לאסוף פרטי קשר בסיסיים כדי לשלוח שאלון במייל. מטרת השאלון (שהינו מפורט) היא איסוף פרטים מלאים בכדי לבסס את האבחנה הקלינית ולהתאים טיפול. טיפול יכול להיות פרטני או קבוצתי. האבחון גם מאפשר לקבוע את מידת הדחיפות בטיפול (לדוגמא זיהוי אובדנות שידרוש טיפול מיידי). 
השאלונים נבנו על גבי (Wordpress (W.P לאחר שליחה הנתונים המלאים עוברים ל- database שנמצא מאחורי login ב W.P וכמו-כן נתונים מקוצרים יותר עוברים ל Google Sheet (מאחורי לוגין ארגוני) ומאפשרים מעבר מהיר ככל הניתן. אין קישור בין השאלון המקוצר למלא ובין שתי המערכות ויש צורך בחיפוש ידני ב W.P. 

מעבר קליני ראשון- האתגר בתפעול הרשימה מתחיל במעבר הקליני הראשון. המעבר הראשון מבוצע ע״י מאבחנת מטעם העמותה. המידע איכותני ברובו ובמעבר  קשה מאוד לאתר באופן מהיר ויעיל מהי התאמת הטיפול, הנכונה לפונה

לדוגמא:

קשה לאתר פניות חדשות

לא ניתן לבצע אבחון מהיר של דחיפות הטיפול

לא ניתן להוסיף הערות על גבי תוצאות השאלון (בפועל מתבצעת עריכה על הטקסט הנתונים שהוזנו ע״י המטופלים)

לא ניתן להוסיף המלצות להמשך טיפול

מעבר קליני שני- מתבצע ע״י מנהלת קלינית בדר״כ בתזמון של פניות המטפלים ומציאת מטופלים או יצירת קבוצת טיפול חדשה ומציאת מועמדים מתאימים. 
בפועל, העבודה האיכותנית קשה ביותר לתפעול על גבי Google Sheet מה גם שאין קישור ישיר לשאלון המלא. 

השמה- מעבר action items אחרי שלב 3 (מעבר קליני שני) בנוגע למטופל למזכירות ותחילת תהליך טיפולי. החל מנקודה זאת נכנסת לשימוש מערכת טיפולוג וכל נתוני הטיפול עוברים לשם. 



הצורך/ הבעיה, כפי שהעלתה העמותה 

יצירת רשימת המתנה "חכמה" של הפונים שמאפשרת יכולות סינון והתאמת מטפל למטופל, יצירת קבוצות טיפוליות ועוד, וחיבור רשימת ההמתנה לאתר הקיים. 



פתרון אופציונלי לבעיה, כפי שהעלתה העמותה
ישנן דרכים רבות לייעל את העבודה עצמה באמצעות ניהול רשימה חכמה, לדוגמא סימון מועמדים לטיפול ויצירת פעולות מהירות (כגון שליחה מהירה של מידע טיפולי או סיווג לקבוצות מתאימות. פעולה מהירה נוספת חשובה היא זיהוי דחיפות ויצירת קשר מהיר עם המטופל)

ניתן לשקול לייעל את העבודה בכל הקשור לתהליך ההשמה ואינטגרציה עם טיפולוג

ניתן לשקול יצירת database לאימון machine learning ואבחון מהיר באמצעות מכונה

ישנה חשיבות גדולה לנושא אבטחת המידע ופרטיות המטופלים, מאופי הנתונים הנשמרים במערכת. 

יש לאפשר מספר הרשאות לצפיה וביצוע פעולות במערכת 



קהל יעד - מי המשתמשים בפתרון

מאבחנים- הפרסונה המרכזית הינה הדמות המאבחנת את נתוני השאלון לצורך סינון והמלצות לטיפול.

פסיכולוגים קליניים- פרסונה אשר מסננת קלינית עבור השמה לטיפול.

מזכירות- ביצוע ההשמה בפועל וקשר עם המטופלים או קופות החולים.



פתרונות רלוונטיים לאתגר הקיימים בשוק

המערכת הפוטנציאלית מזכירה יכולות של CRM והנושא עלה בשיחה עם העמותה. יהיה ניתן לממש חלק גדול מהדרישות בפלטפורמה כגון Salesforce. יחד עם כך יש לקחת בחשבון

פיתוח והטמעה- סביר להניח שיהיה צורך בפיתוח נוסף על גבי מערכות CRM ויהיה צורך באנשי מקצוע שמכירים את התחום

עלויות- עלות פיתוח, הטמעה ושימוש שוטף במערכת. יש גם להניח שלאחר הטמעה מעבר למערכת אחרת יהיה תהליך ארוך וכואב.

אבטחת מידע- מכיוון שמדובר במידע קליני יש לבחון האם ניתן להעלות מידע שכזה למערכת CRM (זה אגב נושא פתוח גם בהקשר של העלאת הנתונים כיום ל Google Sheet)

 פיתוח עתידי- מכיוון שהעמותה צוברת מידע רב ויקר ערך, כל פיתוח עתידי אפשרי (כדוגמת אימון מכונה כיצד לאבחן נתונים ולהמליץ) ידרוש פיתוח נוסף על הנתונים אשר מצטברים ב CRM

מאבחנים- הפרסונה המרכזית הינה הדמות המאבחנת את נתוני השאלון לצורך סינון והמלצות לטיפול.

פסיכולוגים קליניים- פרסונה אשר מסננת קלינית עבור השמה לטיפול.

מזכירות- ביצוע ההשמה בפועל וקשר עם המטופלים או קופות החולים.



פתרונות רלוונטיים לאתגר הקיימים בשוק

המערכת הפוטנציאלית מזכירה יכולות של CRM והנושא עלה בשיחה עם העמותה. יהיה ניתן לממש חלק גדול מהדרישות בפלטפורמה כגון Salesforce. יחד עם כך יש לקחת בחשבון

פיתוח והטמעה- סביר להניח שיהיה צורך בפיתוח נוסף על גבי מערכות CRM ויהיה צורך באנשי מקצוע שמכירים את התחום

עלויות- עלות פיתוח, הטמעה ושימוש שוטף במערכת. יש גם להניח שלאחר הטמעה מעבר למערכת אחרת יהיה תהליך ארוך וכואב.

אבטחת מידע- מכיוון שמדובר במידע קליני יש לבחון האם ניתן להעלות מידע שכזה למערכת CRM (זה אגב נושא פתוח גם בהקשר של העלאת הנתונים כיום ל Google Sheet)

 פיתוח עתידי- מכיוון שהעמותה צוברת מידע רב ויקר ערך, כל פיתוח עתידי אפשרי (כדוגמת אימון מכונה כיצד לאבחן נתונים ולהמליץ) ידרוש פיתוח נוסף על הנתונים אשר מצטברים ב CRM

## System Modules

### Module 1: Patient Referral Intake

Module for receiving initial patient referrals from health funds or private sources. It collects basic contact information to facilitate further communication and questionnaire distribution.

Inputs: Patient's basic contact information (name, email, phone number)
Outputs: Confirmation of referral receipt and initiation of questionnaire distribution

---

### Module 2: Patient Inquiry Reception

משוב על מודול קיים:
מודול לקבלת פניות ראשוניות ממטופלים, איסוף פרטי קשר בסיסיים לצורך שליחת שאלון מפורט בדוא"ל.

קלטים: פרטי קשר של המטופל, מקור הפנייה (קופת חולים או פרטי).
פלטים: אישור קבלת הפנייה, דוא"ל שנשלח עם קישור לשאלון.

תיאור מודול משופר ומפורט:
מודול זה מתוכנן לטפל בפניות ראשוניות ממטופלים ולספק תהליך חלק לאיסוף מידע ראשוני. המודול ישמש כנקודת הקשר הראשונית בין המוסד הרפואי לבין המטופלים הפוטנציאליים, ויבטיח שהמידע הנדרש נאסף בצורה יעילה ומדויקת.

פונקציונליות:
1. קבלת פניות: המודול יקבל פניות ממטופלים דרך טפסים מקוונים, טלפונים או פלטפורמות אחרות.
2. איסוף פרטי קשר: המודול יאסוף פרטי קשר בסיסיים כגון שם מלא, כתובת דוא"ל, מספר טלפון, ומקור הפנייה (לדוגמה, קופת חולים או פנייה פרטית).
3. אימות נתונים: המידע הנאסף יאומת להבטחת דיוק, כולל בדיקת תקינות כתובת הדוא"ל ומספר הטלפון.
4. שליחת דוא"ל: המודול ישלח דוא"ל אוטומטי עם קישור לשאלון מפורט, שיאפשר למטופלים לספק מידע נוסף הנדרש להמשך טיפול.
5. אישור קבלת פנייה: לאחר שליחת הדוא"ל, המטופל יקבל הודעה המאשרת את קבלת הפנייה והמשך התהליך.

שיקולי יישום:
- תאימות לשפות: המודול יתמוך בשפות שונות, כולל עברית, על מנת להבטיח נוחות שימוש לכל המשתמשים.
- אבטחת מידע: יש להבטיח שהמידע האישי של המטופלים מאובטח ומוגן בהתאם לתקנות הפרטיות הרלוונטיות.
- שילוב מערכת: המודול ישתלב עם מערכות קיימות כגון CRM או מערכות ניהול מטופלים, על מנת לייעל את תהליך העבודה ולמנוע כפילויות.

---

### Module 3: Questionnaire Distribution and Collection

Module for sending detailed questionnaires to patients via email and collecting their responses to assess clinical conditions and treatment needs.

Inputs: Patient's email address, detailed clinical questionnaire template
Outputs: Completed patient questionnaires with detailed clinical information

---

### Module 4: Questionnaire Management

Handles the creation, distribution, and collection of detailed questionnaires to gather comprehensive patient data for clinical diagnosis.

Inputs: Patient contact details, questionnaire template.
Outputs: Completed questionnaire data stored in the database.

---

### Module 5: Data Storage and Management

Module for storing patient data securely in a database and ensuring synchronization between a full database and a Google Sheet for quick access.

Inputs: Completed patient questionnaires, patient contact information
Outputs: Stored patient data in a database, summarized data in Google Sheet

---

### Module 6: Data Integration Module

Integrates completed questionnaire data into both a central database and a Google Sheet for quick access.

Inputs: Completed questionnaire data.
Outputs: Stored data in the W.P database and summarized data in Google Sheet.

---

### Module 7: Clinical Assessment and Prioritization

Module for clinicians to assess the clinical data and prioritize treatment urgency, including identifying high-risk patients requiring immediate attention.

Inputs: Patient questionnaire data, clinician assessment criteria
Outputs: Assessment reports with treatment recommendations and urgency levels

---

### Module 8: First Clinical Review

Module for clinical evaluators to assess questionnaire data and determine initial treatment needs and urgency.

Inputs: Completed questionnaire data from W.P database and Google Sheet.
Outputs: Initial treatment recommendations, urgency level identification, and evaluator notes.

---

### Module 9: Patient Treatment Matching

Module for matching patients with appropriate therapists or treatment groups based on clinical assessments and availability.

Inputs: Assessment reports, therapist availability, treatment group criteria
Outputs: Patient-therapist matches and treatment group assignments

---

### Module 10: Second Clinical Review

Facilitates a secondary review by clinical managers to finalize treatment plans and group therapy allocations.

Inputs: Evaluations and recommendations from the first clinical review.
Outputs: Finalized treatment plans, group therapy allocations, and clinical manager notes.

---

### Module 11: Smart Waiting List Management

Creates and manages a smart waiting list that allows filtering and matching of therapists to patients, and forming treatment groups.

Inputs: Patient treatment urgency, therapist availability, and group therapy requirements.
Outputs: Optimized waiting list with matched therapists and treatment group assignments.

---

### Module 12: Smart Waiting List Management

Module to create and manage a dynamic waiting list that allows filtering and quick actions for patient management.

Inputs: Patient data, priority levels, therapist and group availability
Outputs: Filtered and prioritized waiting list with quick action capabilities

---

### Module 13: Integration with Treatment System

Module to transfer patient data to the treatment system (Tipulog) after clinical assessments for treatment initiation.

Inputs: Patient treatment assignment data
Outputs: Transfer of patient data to Tipulog for treatment processing

---

### Module 14: Treatment Assignment and Tracking

Assigns patients to treatment plans and tracks their progress, integrating with the existing Typolog system.

Inputs: Finalized treatment plans, patient and therapist details.
Outputs: Treatment assignments in Typolog system, tracking and progress updates.

---

### Module 15: Machine Learning and Data Analysis

Module for developing machine learning models to assist in rapid clinical assessment and treatment recommendation based on accumulated data.

Inputs: Historical patient data, clinical outcomes
Outputs: Predictive models for clinical assessment and treatment recommendations

---

### Module 16: Machine Learning Integration

Optional module for implementing machine learning to enhance rapid diagnostic capabilities and treatment recommendations.

Inputs: Historical patient data, completed questionnaires.
Outputs: Automated diagnostic insights and treatment suggestions.

---

### Module 17: User Authentication and Authorization

Manages user authentication and authorization levels to ensure secure access to patient data.

Inputs: User credentials, role definitions.
Outputs: Access tokens, user session data, access control enforcement.

---

### Module 18: User Authentication and Authorization

Module for managing user access levels and ensuring secure login for different roles such as assessors, clinical psychologists, and administrative staff.

Inputs: User credentials, role definitions
Outputs: Authenticated user sessions and role-based access control

---

### Module 19: Data Privacy and Security

Ensures the protection of sensitive clinical data through encryption, secure storage, and compliance with data protection regulations.

Inputs: All patient and clinical data.
Outputs: Encrypted data storage, audit logs, compliance reports.

---

### Module 20: Data Privacy and Security Management

Module for ensuring compliance with data privacy regulations and implementing security measures for sensitive clinical data.

Inputs: Patient data, security policies
Outputs: Data encryption, access logs, and privacy compliance reports

---
SystemDesignFormatted:
**System Design Summary**

**1. System Type & Architecture Pattern:**
- **Type:** Clinical Intake and Management System
- **Architecture Pattern:** Modular, Microservices-based architecture to ensure scalability and flexibility. This allows independent deployment of components and integration with external systems.

**2. Key Technologies Recommended:**
- **Frontend:** React.js for a responsive and user-friendly interface.
- **Backend:** Node.js for handling API requests and business logic.
- **Database:** PostgreSQL for structured data and MongoDB for unstructured data.
- **Machine Learning:** TensorFlow for building and deploying predictive models.
- **CRM Integration:** Salesforce for patient relationship management and workflow automation.

**3. Main Components & Their Purpose:**
- **Intake Module:** Collects and stores patient information via dynamic forms.
- **Assessment Module:** Analyzes questionnaire data to determine treatment urgency and recommendations.
- **Matching Engine:** Matches patients with suitable therapists or groups based on assessment results.
- **Admin Dashboard:** Provides a centralized interface for managing appointments, therapists, and patient data.
- **Communication Module:** Facilitates secure messaging between patients and healthcare providers.

**4. Database & Data Strategy:**
- **Primary Database:** PostgreSQL for storing structured patient data and treatment records.
- **Secondary Database:** MongoDB for storing unstructured data such as notes and comments.
- **Data Privacy:** Implement robust encryption and access controls to ensure data security and compliance with HIPAA regulations.
- **Data Integration:** Use ETL processes to sync data between Salesforce and internal databases.

**5. Deployment & Infrastructure:**
- **Cloud Provider:** AWS for scalable and reliable infrastructure.
- **Containerization:** Use Docker to package and deploy applications consistently across environments.
- **Orchestration:** Kubernetes for managing containerized applications and ensuring high availability.
- **CI/CD Pipeline:** Implement Jenkins for automated testing and deployment to streamline updates and maintain system integrity.

This system design aims to streamline the clinical intake process, enhance patient-provider interaction, and ensure secure handling of sensitive data, aligning with the organization's operational needs and strategic goals.

**System Design Summary for Intelligent Filtering System**

**1. System Type & Architecture Pattern**
- **Type**: Intelligent Filtering and Prioritization System
- **Architecture Pattern**: Microservices architecture to ensure scalability, flexibility, and ease of maintenance.

**2. Key Technologies Recommended**
- **Programming Languages**: Python for machine learning components, JavaScript (Node.js) for backend services.
- **Machine Learning Frameworks**: TensorFlow or PyTorch for developing and deploying machine learning models.
- **API Gateway**: AWS API Gateway or Kong for managing API requests.
- **Message Broker**: Apache Kafka for handling asynchronous communication between services.
- **Frontend Framework**: React.js for building a user-friendly interface.

**3. Main Components & Their Purpose**
- **Machine Learning Module**: Analyzes incoming inquiries to filter and prioritize relevant ones based on predefined criteria.
- **User Interface**: Displays inquiries and their relevance scores to operators, allowing them to focus on high-priority tasks.
- **API Layer**: Exposes endpoints for data input and retrieval, enabling seamless integration with existing systems.
- **Notification System**: Alerts operators about high-priority inquiries in real-time.

**4. Database & Data Strategy**
- **Database**: PostgreSQL for structured data storage and retrieval, optimized for complex queries.
- **Data Strategy**: Implement data pipelines using Apache Kafka to ingest, process, and store data efficiently. Utilize a feature store for machine learning input data management.

**5. Deployment & Infrastructure**
- **Cloud Provider**: AWS for its extensive services and scalability options.
- **Containerization & Orchestration**: Docker for packaging microservices and Kubernetes for orchestration, ensuring high availability and scalability.
- **Monitoring & Logging**: Use AWS CloudWatch and ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) for real-time monitoring and logging to quickly identify and resolve issues.
- **CI/CD Pipeline**: Implement Jenkins or GitHub Actions for continuous integration and deployment to ensure rapid iterations and deployment of new features.

This system design aims to reduce operational costs and improve efficiency by enabling the intelligent filtering of inquiries, allowing operators to focus on the most relevant tasks.

**System Design Summary**

**1. System Type & Architecture Pattern**
- The system is a test application designed to evaluate specific functionalities.
- Architecture Pattern: Microservices architecture to ensure scalability, flexibility, and ease of maintenance.

**2. Key Technologies Recommended**
- Backend: Node.js for efficient, scalable server-side logic.
- Frontend: React.js for a dynamic, responsive user interface.
- API Gateway: Express.js to manage API requests and routing.
- Containerization: Docker for consistent development and production environments.
- Orchestration: Kubernetes for container management and service orchestration.

**3. Main Components & Their Purpose**

- **Authentication Service:** Manages user login and security, ensuring authorized access.
- **Business Logic Service:** Handles core functionalities and processes business rules.
- **Data Processing Service:** Manages data validation, transformation, and any batch processing needed.
- **API Gateway:** Coordinates API calls and serves as a single entry point for client interactions.

**4. Database & Data Strategy**
- Database: PostgreSQL for robust, relational data storage.

# System Design for System for tracking patients with endometriosis

**Project Description:** Digital diary for documenting endo pain

**Extended Description:** Women suffering from endo are required to document in a notebook every day the different pains, and it would be successful if there was a convenient documentation app that includes days, hours, and body locations to mark where it hurts to bring this to the doctor during the monthly follow-up.

The system should provide a simple, convenient interface for the patient to document the day and time of the pain, body area, review history, and output reports by week or month for tracking with the doctor.

---

## System Modules

### Module 1: Pain Documentation Interface

This module provides the user with a smart and intuitive tool for managing and documenting daily pain, with an emphasis on a smooth user experience, accuracy, and the highest level of data security.

**Module functionality includes:**

1. **Selecting Date and Time**:
   - An interactive calendar interface that allows precise selection of the date and time of pain occurrence. Includes monthly and yearly views with convenient navigation options.
   - Customization of the time format (12 or 24 hours) with the option for manual input and real-time corrections.

2. **Identifying Pain Area**:
   - An interactive 3D body map for accurate identification of the pain area, including zoom, rotation, and detailed views of body parts.
   - Predefined categories for quick identification of common areas such as head, back, and limbs, with the option to add custom areas.

3. **Pain Intensity and Documentation of Additional Symptoms**:
   - A graphical slider rated from 1 to 10 for measuring pain intensity, with a dynamic and immediate display of the selected value.
   - Custom fields for documenting related symptoms, including verbal descriptions, frequency of occurrence, and relation to daily activities.

4. **Additional Descriptions**:
   - Categories to choose from such as type of pain (sharp, dull, stabbing), duration of pain, triggers, and accompanying symptoms, with the option to add further details.
   - Ability to upload free notes and attachments such as images, medical reports, or additional documents.

5. **Custom Tagging Lists**:
   - Creation and management of custom lists for documenting medications, relief actions, and additional effects.
   - An interface for editing and saving the lists, with options for sharing and collaborating with healthcare professionals.

**Data Management and Analysis:**

- All information is stored in an encrypted database to ensure confidentiality and integrity of personal data.
- Analysis of pain patterns and accompanying symptoms using advanced graphs and statistics, including the generation of visual reports with unique insights.
- A smart alert system for tracking changes in pain patterns and symptoms, with customizable alert conditions.

**Tools for Sharing and Exporting Data:**

- Exporting data to PDF or CSV files for easy and convenient sharing with healthcare professionals.
- Generation of periodic reports summarizing data and key insights, presented in a user-friendly format.
- Option to securely share reports via email, medical management systems, or other digital platforms, with advanced data security.

---

### Module 2: Viewing Pain History

An enhanced and comprehensive description of the module for managing and documenting pain history and additional symptoms:

The module for managing and documenting pain history and additional symptoms is designed to provide users with advanced tools for managing and documenting personal medical information. The module focuses not only on pain but also on documenting additional symptoms such as falls, itching, and sexually transmitted diseases, while providing tools for analyzing data over time and improving quality of life. The module offers a clear and comprehensive picture of the user's symptom and pain patterns, including tools for planning future treatments.

**Detailed Functionality:**

1. **Access to a complete and detailed history of symptom and pain documentation:**  
   - Users can access all their symptom and pain documentation records, organized chronologically by date and time. The system includes advanced filtering options by type of symptom or pain, their intensity, and body location, to allow for quick and focused searches of historical information.

2. **Editing and deleting documentation records:**  
   - Users can edit or delete existing records, with the option to add notes or additional descriptions to improve the accuracy and timeliness of the information. Every action is saved in the change history for tracking purposes.

3. **Advanced statistical analysis tools for symptoms and pain:**  
   - The module offers advanced statistical analyses, including average calculations of symptoms and pain over defined periods, identification of seasonal patterns, and presentation of graphs and visualizations that illustrate the data clearly and understandably.

4. **Tracking insights over time:**  
   - Users receive insights into their symptom and pain patterns over time, including comparisons to previous periods, identification of trends, and the impact of treatments or lifestyle changes.

5. **Preparation for medical appointments:**  
   - The module allows for the creation of summary reports, with the option to customize the format and content to meet the needs of the medical appointment, including history, statistics, and key insights.

**Inputs:**
- User request to view a detailed history of symptoms and pain
- Request to edit or delete an existing record with the option to add notes
- Request to receive advanced statistical analysis based on existing data

**Outputs:**
- Detailed historical records of symptom and pain documentation
- Graphs, statistics, and visual insights
- Customized summary reports for use in medical appointments

**Implementation Considerations:**
- Ensure user privacy and security of medical information through advanced encryption methods and controlled access.
- The interface should be user-friendly and accessible, with an emphasis on a comfortable user experience, including support for multiple languages and adaptation to mobile devices.
- Support for personalizations according to the specific needs of the user, including the option for alerts and customized notifications.

### Module 3: Creating Tracking Reports

This module provides the ability to create tracking reports on a weekly or monthly basis. The reports include detailed information about the recorded pains, with graphical summaries and tables that allow the user and the doctor to get a clear picture of the situation. The module enables the user to download the reports in various formats such as PDF or Excel, and to forward them to the doctor for follow-up meetings. The module also includes options for customizing the reports, such as selecting dates, types of pains, and additional details that may be relevant.

**Inputs:** User request to create a report based on a date range
**Outputs:** Tracking report in the selected format

---

### Module 4: Body Area Management

Body Area Management Module:

This module provides advanced tools for managing and customizing the list of body areas where the user experiences pain. The user can add new areas, edit the names of existing areas, or remove areas that are no longer relevant. The module includes a user-friendly interface that integrates an interactive body map, allowing the user to accurately select the area where they experience pain from a variety of predefined or customized body areas.

This functionality is designed to ensure maximum personalization of the system to the unique needs of each user, enhancing the accuracy and effectiveness of pain documentation and management. The module supports input and editing of area data through an intuitive graphical interface, including support for advanced search and filtering, and provides immediate feedback on changes made.

Inputs: User requests for managing and defining body areas, including adding new areas.
Outputs: Updated and personalized list of areas.

---

### Module 5: User Authentication and Security

Enhanced module description:

The user authentication and personal information security module is responsible for protecting system access and the sensitive information of users. The module allows registration and login to the system using secure username and password, and offers two-factor authentication to add an extra layer of security. Users' personal information is protected through strong encryption and secure storage, in accordance with the highest standards of information security.

As part of improving the user experience and enhancing security, the module also supports authentication through third-party services such as Google Authentication. This feature allows users to securely log into the system using their Google account, utilizing advanced encryption and verification.

User management capabilities include password reset, account recovery, and user profile management. The module ensures that only the authenticated user can access her sensitive data, while maintaining privacy and information security.

Input details: login details, password reset requests, Google authentication requests
Outputs: user authentication status, access to secure data, access approval via Google Authentication

Implementation considerations:
- Ensure that the integration with Google Authentication complies with all required protocols and standards.
- Maintain full compatibility with existing security systems and ensure secure storage of all personal information.
- The two-factor authentication process should be accessible and user-friendly while maintaining a high level of security.

### Module 6: Alerts and Reminders

This module provides the user with alerts and reminders for pain documentation. The user can set daily or weekly reminders to not forget to document the pains she experiences. The module sends real-time alerts to the user's smartphone or via email, according to the user's preferences. This feature helps maintain consistency in documentation and ensures that the data is as comprehensive and accurate as possible. The module also allows the user to set special alerts for doctor appointments or other reminders related to her pain management.

This module provides technical support and customer service for users. It includes a support system with frequently asked questions, guides for using the application, and forms to contact the support team. Users can open support tickets if they encounter technical issues or have questions about using the application. The module allows the support team to track tickets, provide solutions, and respond quickly to user issues. This ensures a smooth and satisfying user experience.

## Data Model

### Data Model 1: Database Schema

CREATE TABLE Users (
    user_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    email VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    two_factor_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    CONSTRAINT chk_email_format CHECK (email LIKE '%_@__%.__%'),
    COMMENT 'Stores user authentication and personal information'
);

CREATE TABLE BodyRegions (
    region_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    region_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    UNIQUE (user_id, region_name),
    COMMENT 'Stores customizable body regions for each user'
);

CREATE TABLE PainRecords (
    record_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    region_id INT NOT NULL,
    pain_date DATE NOT NULL,
    pain_time TIME NOT NULL,
    pain_intensity INT CHECK (pain_intensity BETWEEN 0 AND 10),
    additional_description TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (region_id) REFERENCES BodyRegions(region_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    INDEX (user_id, pain_date, pain_time),
    COMMENT 'Records daily pain entries with details for each user'
);

CREATE TABLE PainHistory (
    history_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    record_id INT NOT NULL,
    action_type ENUM('view', 'edit', 'delete') NOT NULL,
    action_timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (record_id) REFERENCES PainRecords(record_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    COMMENT 'Logs user actions on pain records for historical tracking'
);

CREATE TABLE Reports (
    report_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    report_type ENUM('weekly', 'monthly') NOT NULL,
    start_date DATE NOT NULL,
    end_date DATE NOT NULL,
    report_format ENUM('PDF', 'Excel') NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    COMMENT 'Stores generated reports for pain tracking over specified periods'
);

CREATE TABLE Reminders (
    reminder_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    reminder_type ENUM('daily', 'weekly', 'special') NOT NULL,
    reminder_time TIME NOT NULL,
    reminder_message VARCHAR(255),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    COMMENT 'Stores user-defined reminders for pain documentation and appointments'
);

CREATE TABLE SupportTickets (
    ticket_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    issue_description TEXT NOT NULL,
    status ENUM('open', 'in_progress', 'resolved', 'closed') DEFAULT 'open',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    COMMENT 'Tracks support tickets submitted by users for technical assistance'
);

CREATE INDEX idx_user_id ON PainRecords(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_pain_date_time ON PainRecords(pain_date, pain_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_report_user ON Reports(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_reminder_user ON Reminders(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_ticket_user ON SupportTickets(user_id);

SystemDesignFormatted:
**System Design Summary for Pain Tracking Application**

**1. System Type & Architecture Pattern**
- **Type**: Mobile Application
- **Architecture Pattern**: Client-Server Architecture
  - The application will use a client-server model to separate a thin mobile client from a robust backend service.

**2. Key Technologies Recommended**
- **Mobile Platform**: Flutter for cross-platform development (iOS and Android).
- **Backend**: Node.js with Express for handling API requests and business logic.
- **Database**: MongoDB for flexible schema and ease in handling varying data structures.
- **Authentication**: OAuth 2.0 for secure login and user management.
- **APIs**: RESTful APIs for client-server communication.

**3. Main Components & Their Purpose**
- **Mobile Application**: User interface allowing patients to log pain data including time, date, and body location.
- **Backend Server**: Handles data processing, storage, authentication, and provides APIs for mobile app interaction.
- **Admin Dashboard**: Web-based interface for medical professionals to review patient data and trends.
- **Notification Service**: Sends reminders to patients for data entry and follow-up appointments.

**4. Database & Data Strategy**
- **Database**: 
  - MongoDB for storing patients' pain logs, user profiles, and historical data.
  - Flexible schema design to accommodate future data types or changes.
- **Data Strategy**:
  - Use of indexes to optimize query performance for retrieving patient logs.
  - Implement data encryption for sensitive information to ensure patient privacy and compliance with health data regulations.

**5. Deployment & Infrastructure**
- **Cloud Hosting**: AWS or Google Cloud Platform for scalability and reliability.
- **Containerization**: Use Docker for consistent environment setup and deployment.
- **CI/CD Pipeline**: Implement automated testing and deployment using tools like Jenkins or GitHub Actions.
- **Monitoring & Logging**: Utilize services like AWS CloudWatch or ELK Stack for monitoring application health and performance.

This system design ensures a user-friendly experience for patients while providing reliable data management and insights for healthcare providers. The choice of technologies and architecture supports scalability, security, and ease of maintenance.

SystemDesignFormatted:
**System Design Summary for Sentence Transformation Project**

1. **System Type & Architecture Pattern**
   - **Type**: Natural Language Processing (NLP) Application
   - **Architecture Pattern**: Microservices architecture to ensure scalability, flexibility, and independent deployment.

2. **Key Technologies Recommended**
   - **Programming Language**: Python (for NLP capabilities)
   - **NLP Library**: SpaCy or NLTK for semantic analysis and text processing.
   - **Machine Learning Framework**: TensorFlow or PyTorch for custom models if needed.
   - **Web Framework**: Flask or FastAPI for developing RESTful APIs.
   - **Scoring System**: Custom algorithm for scoring sentences, potentially leveraging cloud-based AI services like AWS Comprehend.

3. **Main Components & Their Purpose**
   - **Input Processing Service**: Takes user input, processes the text, and prepares it for analysis.
   - **Sentence Analysis Module**: Analyzes the original sentence to understand its context and generate a related sentence.
   - **Scoring Engine**: Calculates scores based on predefined criteria, translating these scores into dollar values.
   - **User Interface**: Web-based UI for users to input sentences and view results.
   - **Payment Processing Service**: Manages transaction and payment distribution based on scores.

4. **Database & Data Strategy**
   - **Database Type**: NoSQL (e.g., MongoDB) for flexibility and scalability in handling diverse data types.
   - **Data Strategy**: Store user inputs, generated sentences, scores, and transaction logs. Ensure data is anonymized and securely managed.

5. **Deployment & Infrastructure**
   - **Cloud Provider**: AWS or Google Cloud Platform for scalable and resilient infrastructure.
   - **Containerization**: Use Docker for containerizing services to ensure consistent environments across development, testing, and production.
   - **Orchestration**: Kubernetes for managing containers, scaling, and facilitating CI/CD pipelines.
   - **Monitoring & Logging**: Implement monitoring using Prometheus and logging with ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to maintain system health and performance.

This design ensures a robust, scalable system that can handle dynamic user interactions and process complex language tasks efficiently.

SystemDesign:
# System Design for Digital system for monitoring at-risk youth

**Project Description:** Digital system for tracking at-risk youth, allowing the management of an information system instead of WhatsApp currently, in a youth boarding school of youth who use drugs

**Extended Description:** Digital system for monitoring at-risk youth, allowing for managing an information system instead of WhatsApp currently, in a youth boarding school for youth using drugs. The system needs to generate tickets such as: 
Was the youth seen smoking? 
Did the youth steal? 
Did the youth behave well? 
Did they eat in the dining room? 
Also, to connect the information with the personal information of each youth from the Salesforce system.

---

## System Modules

### Module 1: User Management and Authentication Module

This module is designed for managing users and authenticating their identities within the system. The module allows creation, editing, and deletion of user profiles, including staff and educational personnel in the boarding school. Additionally, the module provides a username and password-based authentication system, as well as support for two-factor authentication to ensure information security. The module integrates with existing identity management systems and can import data from external systems like Salesforce. Users can change their password or retrieve it in case of forgetfulness. The module ensures secure and managed access to all functions and information in the system.

Inputs: User details such as username, password, and role in the system.
Outputs: User access authentication, role-based permissions, and an activity log for usage monitoring.

---

### Module 2: Ticket Creation Module

This module allows for the creation and tracking of tickets related to youth behavior in the boarding school. Various events can be documented, such as smoking, theft, good behavior, or eating in the dining hall. Each ticket includes detailed information about the date, time, and location of the event, along with notes from the ticket creator. The module enables updating the ticket status, adding additional notes, and closing tickets after handling. It integrates with the Salesforce system to enrich personal information for each youth. Users can generate statistical reports based on the collected data for data-driven decision-making.

Inputs: Event details like date, time, location, and type of behavior.
Outputs: Documented and updated tickets, statistical reports, and updated personal information regarding the youth.

---

### Module 3: Salesforce Integration Module

This module provides seamless integration with the Salesforce system, allowing access to personal information about each child in the boarding school. The module retrieves data such as contact details, event history, and a general overview of the child. It allows updating information in Salesforce according to changes and new cases documented in the tracking system. The module uses Salesforce's API to securely and transparently transfer data, ensuring that the information is always updated and synchronized between the systems. This helps users understand the relationship between behavioral events and the child's extended personal information.

Inputs:
- Requests for personal information about children
- New events for documentation.

Outputs:
- Updated and synchronized personal information
- Event documentation in the Salesforce system.

**Inputs:** - Requests for personal information about children
- New events for documentation.

**Outputs:** - Updated and synchronized personal information
- Event documentation in the Salesforce system.

---

### Module 4: Data Reporting and Analysis Module

This module allows users to create detailed reports and statistical analyses based on the data collected in the system. Reports can be generated daily, weekly, or monthly, showcasing trends in youth behavior, exceptional events, and changes in incident frequency. The module offers graphical tools for data presentation, such as graphs, tables, and diagrams. Users can select specific criteria for analysis and customize the reports for different needs. The module provides insights for improving decision-making and understanding problematic situations. The reports serve as an essential tool for management and education staff in the boarding school.

**Inputs:** Ticket data, selected analysis criteria.
**Outputs:** Graphical reports, statistical analyses, insights for action.

---

### Module 5: Alerts and Notifications Module

This module is responsible for creating and sending alerts and notifications to users in the system according to certain events. For example, when a new ticket is created or a change occurs in the status of an existing ticket, the module sends an alert to relevant users. It allows customization of alerts according to user preferences, including choosing between real-time or periodic alerts. The module supports sending messages via email or SMS, ensuring that information reaches the appropriate parties in real-time. This helps users stay updated and respond quickly to critical events.

Inputs: New events, ticket changes, user preferences.
Outputs: Customized real-time alerts and notifications.

---

### Module 6: Permissions and Security Management Module

This module provides a permissions and information security management system for all users in the system. It allows defining roles and specific access permissions for each user according to their role in the organization. The module ensures that sensitive information is accessible only to authorized entities. It tracks user actions and generates activity logs for security and audit purposes. The system includes features such as encryption, access control, and password management. The module integrates with existing security systems and ensures that the system complies with the strictest security standards.

Inputs: User details and roles, access requests and tasks.
Outputs: Customized access permissions, activity logs, information security.

---

### Module 7: Support and Customer Service Module

This module provides technical support and customer service for system users. It includes a ticket management system, allowing users to report issues, ask questions, and receive technical support. The tickets are documented in the system, and users can track the status of their requests. The module enables the support team to manage tickets efficiently, assign staff to handle requests, and deliver quick solutions. It also includes a knowledge base with answers to frequently asked questions and guides for using the system. The module aims to enhance the user experience and ensure continuous support.

Inputs: User requests, questions, and technical issues.
Outputs: Solutions and answers to requests, guides, and technical support.

---

### Module 8: Main User Interface Module

This module provides the main interface where users can access all the system functions. It includes menus, dashboards, and navigation options that allow easy and quick access to various features. The interface is designed to be user-friendly, with a clean and clear design, allowing users to perform tasks intuitively. The interface supports customization, so each user can configure their workspace according to their preferences. It integrates with various modules and provides a coherent and unified user experience. Each user accesses relevant information and reports based on the permissions granted to them.

Inputs: User requests for navigation and action execution.
Outputs: Intuitive user interface, access to customized functions and reports.

---

## Data Model

### Data Model 1: Database Schema

CREATE TABLE Users (
    user_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    role VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    two_factor_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    CONSTRAINT chk_role CHECK (role IN ('Staff', 'Educator', 'Admin'))
);

CREATE TABLE UserProfiles (
    profile_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    first_name VARCHAR(100),
    last_name VARCHAR(100),
    email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
    phone VARCHAR(15),
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

CREATE TABLE Tickets (
    ticket_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    event_date DATETIME NOT NULL,
    location VARCHAR(255),
    behavior_type VARCHAR(100),
    notes TEXT,
    status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Open',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    CONSTRAINT chk_status CHECK (status IN ('Open', 'In Progress', 'Closed'))
);

CREATE TABLE SalesforceIntegration (
    integration_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    contact_info JSON,
    event_history JSON,
    last_sync TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

CREATE TABLE Reports (
    report_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    report_type VARCHAR(50),
    criteria JSON,
    generated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

CREATE TABLE Alerts (
    alert_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    ticket_id INT,
    alert_type VARCHAR(50),
    message TEXT,
    sent_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (ticket_id) REFERENCES Tickets(ticket_id) ON DELETE SET NULL
);

CREATE TABLE Permissions (
    permission_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    permission_type VARCHAR(50),
    granted_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

CREATE TABLE ActivityLogs (
    log_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    action VARCHAR(255),
    performed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

CREATE TABLE SupportTickets (
    support_ticket_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    issue_description TEXT,
    status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Open',
    assigned_to INT,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (assigned_to) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    CONSTRAINT chk_support_status CHECK (status IN ('Open', 'In Progress', 'Resolved'))
);

CREATE TABLE KnowledgeBase (
    article_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    title VARCHAR(255),
    content TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_username ON Users(username);
CREATE INDEX idx_tickets_event_date ON Tickets(event_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_alerts_sent_at ON Alerts(sent_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_support_tickets_status ON SupportTickets(status);

-- Comments explaining the purpose of each table and column
-- Users: Stores user credentials and roles
-- UserProfiles: Stores additional user profile information
-- Tickets: Records behavioral events and their statuses
-- SalesforceIntegration: Manages integration data with Salesforce
-- Reports: Stores generated report data and criteria
-- Alerts: Manages alerts and notifications for users
-- Permissions: Manages user permissions and access rights
-- ActivityLogs: Tracks user actions for auditing
-- SupportTickets: Manages user support requests and their statuses
-- KnowledgeBase: Stores articles and guides for user support

---
SystemDesignFormatted:
**System Design Summary for Digital Monitoring System for At-Risk Youth**

**1. System Type & Architecture Pattern:**
- **Type:** Digital Monitoring and Management System.
- **Architecture Pattern:** Microservices architecture to ensure scalability, modularity, and ease of integration with existing systems like Salesforce.

**2. Key Technologies Recommended:**
- **Backend:** Node.js with Express for RESTful API development.
- **Frontend:** React.js for a responsive and user-friendly interface.
- **Integration:** Salesforce API for seamless data synchronization.
- **Authentication:** OAuth 2.0 for secure access control.

**3. Main Components & Their Purpose:**
- **User Interface (UI):** Allows staff to interact with the system, create, and view tickets related to youth behaviors.
- **Ticket Management Service:** Handles creation, updating, and querying of behavior tickets (e.g., smoking, theft, good behavior).
- **Data Synchronization Service:** Integrates with Salesforce to pull and update personal information about each youth.
- **Notification Service:** Sends alerts and updates to staff regarding ticket status or urgent matters.

**4. Database & Data Strategy:**
- **Database Type:** NoSQL database (e.g., MongoDB) for flexible schema design and scalability.
- **Data Strategy:** Ensure data consistency and integrity between the system and Salesforce using regular synchronization processes. Implement data encryption and anonymization to protect sensitive information.

**5. Deployment & Infrastructure:**
- **Cloud Provider:** AWS for robust infrastructure, utilizing services such as EC2 for hosting, S3 for storage, and RDS for database management.
- **Containerization:** Use Docker for deploying microservices to ensure consistency across environments.
- **CI/CD Pipeline:** Implement Jenkins or GitHub Actions for continuous integration and deployment to streamline updates and feature releases.

This system aims to replace existing communication methods like WhatsApp, providing a structured and secure platform for monitoring and managing information related to at-risk youth. The integration with Salesforce ensures comprehensive insight into each individual’s situation, aiding in better decision-making and support strategies.

SystemDesignFormatted:
**System Design Summary for Patient Reminder and Interaction System**

**1. System Type & Architecture Pattern:**
- **Type:** A mobile and web-based application designed for diabetic patients.
- **Architecture Pattern:** Microservices architecture to ensure scalability and maintainability, allowing independent development and deployment of components.

**2. Key Technologies Recommended:**
- **Frontend:** React Native for mobile applications and React.js for web applications to provide a responsive user interface.
- **Backend:** Node.js with Express for RESTful API development, ensuring high performance and scalability.
- **Real-time Communication:** WebSocket for real-time updates on nearby patients and interaction requests.
- **Music Integration:** Integration with a music streaming API (e.g., Spotify) for personalized music reminders.

**3. Main Components & Their Purpose:**
- **User Management Service:** Handles user registration, authentication, and profile management.
- **Reminder Service:** Manages scheduling and sending reminders with integrated music playback.
- **Nearby Patients Service:** Identifies and displays nearby diabetic patients, facilitating interaction and support.
- **Medication Request Service:** Allows users to request medications from nearby patients, enhancing community support.
- **Notification Service:** Sends push notifications for reminders and interaction requests.

**4. Database & Data Strategy:**
- **Database:** MongoDB for flexible data storage, accommodating user profiles, reminders, and interaction logs.
- **Data Strategy:** Implement a NoSQL database to handle unstructured data efficiently, with a focus on scalability and performance. Use data encryption for sensitive information to ensure patient privacy.

**5. Deployment & Infrastructure:**
- **Cloud Provider:** AWS or Azure for hosting services, ensuring high availability and scalability.
- **Containerization:** Docker for containerizing microservices, facilitating easy deployment and management.
- **CI/CD Pipeline:** Implement a continuous integration and deployment pipeline using tools like Jenkins or GitHub Actions for automated testing and deployment.
- **Monitoring & Logging:** Use tools like Prometheus and Grafana for monitoring system performance and logging for troubleshooting.

This design aims to create a supportive environment for diabetic patients, enhancing their quality of life through reminders and community interaction.

Title: אמץ חתול
Description: אפליקצייה שעוזר לחתולים חסרי בית למצוא משפחה מאמצת
ExtendedDescription:
אפליקציית “חתול לבית” נועדה לתת לחתולים משוטטים או כאלה שננטשו הזדמנות אמיתית למציאת בית חם ואוהב. האפליקציה מחברת בין עמותות, מתנדבים ולקוחות פרטיים שמעוניינים לאמץ, ומפשטת את כל תהליך ההתאמה בעזרת ממשק פשוט ונגיש. כל חתול מקבל כרטיס אישי עם תמונה, גיל, מצב רפואי, אופי והתאמות מיוחדות כמו האם הוא מסתדר עם ילדים או עם בעלי חיים נוספים.

מעבר להצגת החתולים עצמם, האפליקציה משתמשת במנוע התאמה חכם: המשתמש מסמן את העדפותיו – סוג בית, ניסיון קודם עם חתולים, זמני שהייה בבית ועוד – והמערכת מציעה את החתולים שמתאימים ביותר לאורח חייו. כך גם חתולים ביישנים, מבוגרים או כאלה עם צרכים רפואיים מקבלים חשיפה שמתאימה להם.

הפלטפורמה כוללת גם מערכת התראות לעמותות, כך שהן יכולות לעקוב אחרי בקשות אימוץ בזמן אמת, לקבוע פגישות היכרות ולהעביר עדכונים על מצב החתול. בנוסף, האפליקציה מציעה מאגר ידע קצר שמסביר כיצד להתכונן לאימוץ, איך לעזור לחתול להסתגל, ומהם הצעדים הראשונים בתהליך.

באמצעות שילוב של טכנולוגיה וחמלה, “חתול לבית” מצמצמת את המרחק בין חתולים שמחפשים אהבה לבין משפחות שמחפשות חבר חדש לחיים.
SystemDesign:
# System Design for Adopt a cat

**Project Description:** An application that helps homeless cats find adoptive families

**Extended Description:** The "Cat to Home" application is designed to give stray or abandoned cats a real opportunity to find a warm and loving home. The app connects between organizations, volunteers, and private clients interested in adopting, simplifying the entire matching process with a simple and accessible interface. Each cat receives a personal card with a photo, age, medical condition, personality, and special adaptations such as whether it gets along with children or other animals.

In addition to showcasing the cats themselves, the app uses a smart matching engine: the user marks their preferences – type of home, previous experience with cats, time spent at home, and more – and the system suggests the cats that best fit their lifestyle. This way, shy, older cats, or those with medical needs also receive exposure that suits them.

The platform also includes a notification system for organizations, allowing them to track adoption requests in real-time, schedule meet-and-greet appointments, and provide updates on the cat's status. Additionally, the app offers a brief knowledge base explaining how to prepare for adoption, how to help a cat adjust, and what the first steps in the process are.

By combining technology and compassion, "Cat to Home" reduces the distance between cats seeking love and families looking for a new friend for life.

---

## System Modules

### Module 1: User Registration Module

This module is responsible for the user registration process for the "Cat at Home" application. Users can register using an email address and password, or through a social media account. The module includes data validation to ensure that the entered details are correct, as well as security checks to protect users' personal information. After registration, the user receives a confirmation message via email. The module also allows users to recover passwords in case of forgetfulness. This process includes sending a password reset link to the user's email. The module is integrated with the application's database to securely store user details.

**Inputs:** Email address, password, social media details (if required)
**Outputs:** Registration confirmation message, password reset link (if required)

---

### Module 2: User Profile Module

This module allows users to manage their personal profile within the application. Users can update their personal information, such as name, address, phone number, and adoption preferences. The module includes a user-friendly interface that displays all information clearly and allows for easy editing. Additionally, the module offers users the option to add pictures of themselves or the cats they have adopted. The information is stored in the database and updated in real-time, so users can see the changes immediately. The module also provides the option to delete the profile if the user wishes to stop using the application.

**Inputs:** Profile data (name, address, phone, adoption preferences)
**Outputs:** Success message on profile update, updated profile data

---

### Module 3: Cat Card Module

This module displays the cat cards available for adoption. Each cat receives a personal card that includes a picture, age, medical condition, personality, and special adaptations. The module allows users to filter the cats by various criteria such as age, breed, health status, and more. Users can click on a cat card to see more details, including health history, recommendations from organizations, and additional photos. The module also includes an option to share cat cards on social media to increase the visibility of cats in need of a home. All information is stored and managed in the application's database.

Inputs: Criteria for filtering cats (age, breed, health status)
Outputs: List of suitable cat cards, selected cat details

---

### Module 4: Matching Engine Module

This module is responsible for matching cats to users based on their personal preferences. Users can input their preferences, such as:
- Type of home
- Previous experience with cats
- Time spent at home

The module uses smart algorithms to analyze the data and suggest the most suitable cats for each user. The engine also takes into account parameters such as the cat's personality and special needs, ensuring that even shy cats or those with medical issues receive appropriate exposure. The module is integrated with the Cat Card module to present the results in a user-friendly and clear manner.

**Inputs:** User preferences (type of home, experience, time spent)
**Outputs:** List of suitable cats, details of suggested cats

---

### Module 5: Adoption Alerts Module for Nonprofits

This module allows nonprofits to track adoption requests in real-time. When a user submits a request to adopt a cat, the module sends an alert to the relevant nonprofit. Nonprofits can view all received requests, schedule meet-and-greet appointments with potential adopters, and update the status of the cat in the system. The module includes a management interface that displays all requests in an organized manner, with options to filter and search requests by cats or users. The module also allows nonprofits to send updates to users about the status of the cats, ensuring that communication remains clear and efficient.

**Inputs:** Adoption requests from cats, user details
**Outputs:** Alerts to nonprofits, list of adoption requests

---

### Module 6: Knowledge Base Module

This module provides users with important information about the adoption process and preparation for adopting a cat. The module includes articles, tips, and guidelines on how to prepare for adoption, how to help the cat adjust to its new home, and what the first steps in the process are. Users can search for information by topics such as medical care, cat behavior, and home preparation. The module also includes an option to ask questions and receive answers from experts in the field. All information is stored in an organized manner and is regularly updated to ensure that users receive the most current and accurate information.

---

### Module 7: Meeting Management Module

This module allows users to schedule introductory meetings with cats they are interested in adopting. Users can select the cat, choose a date and time, and send a meeting request to the organization. The module includes a calendar that displays the organization's availability, so users can select a convenient time. After scheduling the meeting, the module sends a confirmation to the user and the organization, and updates the system accordingly. The module also allows organizations to manage the meetings, view all scheduled meetings, and make changes if necessary. All information is stored in the application's database.

Inputs: Cat details, date and time for the meeting
Outputs: Meeting confirmation, meeting system update

---

### Module 8: Feedback Management Module

This module allows users to provide feedback on the cats they have adopted and the adoption process itself. Users can rate the cat, write reviews, and share their experiences with others. The module includes a user-friendly interface that presents all feedback in an organized manner, so that organizations can view the feedback and improve the adoption process accordingly. The module also allows organizations to respond to feedback, ensuring open communication with the adopter. All feedback is stored in the database, enabling the system to analyze the data and enhance the services offered.

Inputs: Cat Rating, User Reviews
Outputs: Confirmation of Feedback Receipt, Update of Feedback System

---

## Data Model

### Data Model 1: Database Schema

CREATE TABLE Users (
    user_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    social_network_id VARCHAR(255),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    -- Foreign key to social networks table if needed
    -- FOREIGN KEY (social_network_id) REFERENCES SocialNetworks(id),
    CONSTRAINT chk_email_format CHECK (email LIKE '%_@__%.__%'),
    COMMENT 'Stores user registration details including email and password or social network ID'
);

CREATE TABLE UserProfile (
    profile_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(255),
    address VARCHAR(255),
    phone VARCHAR(20),
    adoption_preferences TEXT,
    profile_picture VARCHAR(255),
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    COMMENT 'Stores personal profile information of users'
);

CREATE TABLE Cats (
    cat_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(255),
    age INT,
    breed VARCHAR(255),
    health_status VARCHAR(255),
    personality TEXT,
    special_needs TEXT,
    picture VARCHAR(255),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    COMMENT 'Stores information about cats available for adoption'
);

CREATE TABLE AdoptionRequests (
    request_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    cat_id INT NOT NULL,
    request_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    status ENUM('Pending', 'Approved', 'Rejected') DEFAULT 'Pending',
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (cat_id) REFERENCES Cats(cat_id),
    COMMENT 'Stores adoption requests made by users for specific cats'
);

CREATE TABLE Meetings (
    meeting_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    cat_id INT NOT NULL,
    meeting_date DATETIME NOT NULL,
    status ENUM('Scheduled', 'Completed', 'Cancelled') DEFAULT 'Scheduled',
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (cat_id) REFERENCES Cats(cat_id),
    COMMENT 'Stores scheduled meetings between users and cats for adoption'
);

CREATE TABLE Feedback (
    feedback_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    cat_id INT NOT NULL,
    rating INT CHECK (rating BETWEEN 1 AND 5),
    comments TEXT,
    feedback_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (cat_id) REFERENCES Cats(cat_id),
    COMMENT 'Stores feedback from users about cats and the adoption process'
);

CREATE TABLE KnowledgeBase (
    article_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    category VARCHAR(255),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    COMMENT 'Stores articles and information related to cat adoption and care'
);

CREATE TABLE Notifications (
    notification_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT,
    request_id INT,
    message TEXT NOT NULL,
    sent_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (request_id) REFERENCES AdoptionRequests(request_id),
    COMMENT 'Stores notifications sent to users and organizations regarding adoption requests'
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON Users(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_profile_user_id ON UserProfile(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_cats_age ON Cats(age);
CREATE INDEX idx_adoption_requests_user_id ON AdoptionRequests(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_adoption_requests_cat_id ON AdoptionRequests(cat_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_meetings_user_id ON Meetings(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_meetings_cat_id ON Meetings(cat_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_feedback_user_id ON Feedback(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_feedback_cat_id ON Feedback(cat_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_knowledge_base_category ON KnowledgeBase(category);
CREATE INDEX idx_notifications_user_id ON Notifications(user_id);

### סיכום עיצוב מערכת עבור אפליקציית "חתול לבית"

1. **סוג מערכת ודפוס ארכיטקטורה**
   - מערכת מבוססת ענן עם ארכיטקטורת Microservices.
   - שימוש ב-API RESTful לתקשורת בין רכיבי המערכת.

2. **טכנולוגיות מרכזיות מומלצות**
   - **Frontend**: React Native לפיתוח אפליקציה ניידת.
   - **Backend**: Node.js עם Express.js לניהול בקשות.
   - **Database**: MongoDB לאחסון נתונים גמיש וקל לשימוש.
   - **Machine Learning**: TensorFlow או Scikit-learn למנוע ההתאמה החכם.

3. **רכיבים עיקריים ומטרותיהם**
   - **ממשק משתמש**: מאפשר למשתמשים לאמץ חתולים, לסמן העדפות ולצפות בכרטיסי חתולים.
   - **מנוע התאמה**: מנתח את העדפות המשתמש ומציע חתולים מתאימים.
   - **מערכת התראות**: מעדכנת עמותות על בקשות אימוץ ומפגשים.
   - **מאגר ידע**: מספק מידע למשתמשים על תהליך האימוץ והכנה.

4. **אסטרטגיית נתונים ומסד נתונים**
   - שימוש ב-MongoDB לאחסון נתוני חתולים, משתמשים, והעדפות.
   - גיבוי נתונים באופן קבוע ושימוש ב-NoSQL לאחסון נתונים לא מובנים.

5. **פריסה ותשתית**
   - פריסה על פלטפורמת AWS או Google Cloud לצורך Scalability.
   - שימוש ב-Docker לקונטיינריזציה של רכיבי המערכת.
   - ניהול תהליכי CI/CD באמצעות Jenkins או GitHub Actions להבטחת איכות הקוד.

באמצעות עיצוב זה, אפליקציית "חתול לבית" תוכל לספק חווית משתמש חלקה ויעילה, תוך שמירה על גמישות ויכולת התרחבות בעתיד.

Title: מודעות הורית ללקות
Description: אפליקציה להעלאת מודעות הורים ללקויות למידה אצל ילדים
ExtendedDescription:
אפליקציית “לומדים ביחד” נועדה לסייע להורים לזהות מוקדם סימנים ללקויות למידה ולהבין טוב יותר את הצרכים הייחודיים של ילדיהם. האפליקציה מאפשרת להורים לקבל מידע אמין, ברור ונגיש – בלי עומס מקצועי ובלי צורך בניסיון קודם בתחום החינוך המיוחד.

כבר במסך הבית מוצגים כלים פשוטים להערכה ראשונית: שאלונים ידידותיים, דוגמאות להתנהגויות שכיחות אצל ילדים עם לקויות שונות, וכן הסברים על מתי כדאי להתייעץ עם גורם מקצועי. כל הסבר מלווה בדוגמאות יומיומיות ממצבים בבית ובבית הספר, כדי לעזור להורים להבין מה הם רואים בפועל ולא רק לקרוא תיאוריה.

האפליקציה מציעה גם ספריית תוכן רחבה הכוללת סרטונים קצרים, טיפים מעשיים, וכתבות המתעדכנות באופן שוטף בנושאים כגון דיסלקסיה, הפרעת קשב, קשיים בשפה, ועיבוד חושי. בנוסף, קיימת מערכת התראות שמזכירה להורים לבדוק התקדמות, לשמור הערות ולתעד תצפיות – מידע שיכול להיות חשוב מאוד בפגישה עם מורים, יועצים או מאבחנים.

לצד כל אלה, קיימת בקהילה מקום לשיח בטוח שבו הורים יכולים לשאול שאלות, לשתף ניסיון ולקבל תמיכה מאחרים המתמודדים עם מצבים דומים. המטרה של “לומדים ביחד” היא להפוך את ההורים לשותפים פעילים בדרך לזיהוי, הבנה ומתן מענה נכון לילדיהם – מוקדם, רגיש ומבוסס ידע.
